task_completion.description: Access the Floreant POS Back Office
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Floreant POS Back Office dashboard or main menu is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Open the Order Type creation window
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog or window specifically for creating or editing an Order Type is open in the application.

task_completion.description: Enter 'Curbside' as the Order Type name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Curbside' (case-sensitive) is typed into the Name field of the Order Type configuration window.

task_completion.description: Disable Show Table Selection and Show Guest Selection
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkboxes for 'Show Table Selection' and 'Show Guest Selection' are left unchecked (empty) in the Order Type window.

task_completion.description: Enable Require Customer Data and Prepaid
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkboxes for 'Require Customer Data' and 'Prepaid' are checked in the Order Type window.
The new Order Type 'Curbside' is saved, typically evidenced by it appearing in the list of configured Order Types or the configuration window closing after clicking Save/OK.

integrity.description: The agent used the Floreant POS graphical user interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Floreant POS application windows, menus, and dialogs, rather than terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to mock the results
integrity.visual_evidence: The configurations are made within the actual Floreant POS Order Type dialog, not typed into a text file or document.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Floreant POS software
integrity.visual_evidence: The application interface matches a Java-based POS system with standard UI elements, not a mockup or different application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Floreant POS is a real Java-based restaurant POS system. The Order Type configuration window contains specific checkboxes: 'Show Table Selection', 'Show Guest Selection', 'Require Customer Data', and 'Prepaid'. For the 'Curbside' order type, 'Show Table Selection' and 'Show Guest Selection' must be unchecked, while 'Require Customer Data' and 'Prepaid' must be checked. The agent must use the GUI to configure this, rather than modifying the underlying Apache Derby database directly.
